name: Mexican Fiesta Tart
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Shredded Monterey Jack cheese with jalapeno
2 cups Shredded sharp Cheddar cheese (8 ounces)
4 large tomatoes (1 1/4 pounds), cored, seeded, and cut into 3/4-inch cubes (3 cups)
1/2 large red or green bell pepper, in thin strips
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F. On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough to a 25-inch round. Transfer to a 12-inch tart pan with removable bottom. Trim edges; prick the bottom with the tines of a fork. Line pastry shell with pastry weights, dried beans, or raw rice. Bake for 15 minutes.
Remove foil with weights. Bake an additional 5 to 6 minutes, or until the pastry starts to turn golden. Brush with egg white and bake for 1 minute longer. Cool completely on a wire rack.
In a large bowl, toss together the cheeses. Sprinkle half the cheese mixture over the bottom of the cooled tart shell. Top with a layer of tomatoes, then half the onions, zucchini, pepper, olives, remaining onions, and remaining cheese. Bake for 20 minutes or until heated through. Cool 15 minutes on a wire rack before removing the outer ring. Serve warm.
Makes 10 servings.
